prizes.label: Overall Pool Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings, all four majors

prizes.label: Group Champion
prizes.desc: Most cumulative earnings within your group of 8

usOpenRound.label: Final Results · U.S. Open · Wyndham Clark wins

participants.name: Ali Pejman
participants.blurbShort: Wyndham Clark just made Ali Pejman $4.5 million richer and vaulted him to first overall. The man who spent two majors watching Clark do nothing is now laughing all the way to Royal Birkdale.
participants.blurb: Ali Pejman drafted Wyndham Clark at the start of the season, watched him miss the Masters cut and skip the PGA Championship, and quietly kept the faith. Shinnecock Hills repaid that faith with the largest single-player payout in pool history. Clark's $4.5M, plus Tyrrell Hatton (T7, $617K) and Corey Conners (T23, $181K), gave Ali a staggering $5.4M US Open total. He now leads the overall pool by $1.5M over Arif Nuraney. Heading into The Open at Royal Birkdale: Ali needs to stay warm and not do anything stupid like draft Jason Day. His lead is large enough to survive one mediocre major. Two would be pushing it. One would need to be truly catastrophic.

participants.name: Arif Nuraney
participants.blurbShort: Still second overall despite getting almost nothing from the US Open. Rory won the Masters, Aaron Rai won the PGA — you're running on fumes from two majors ago and somehow still in contention. Respect.
participants.blurb: Arif Nuraney is the pool's version of a team that leads the league on carry-forward points while their current form is quietly dreadful. Rory McIlroy finished T32 at Shinnecock ($128K), Aaron Rai T11 ($405K), Ben Griffin T17 ($280K) — decent but not dominant. The US Open added $888K, which sounds fine until you realize Ali Pejman just pocketed $5.4M in the same event. Arif's $1.55M lead over third place is real, but so is the gap behind him. At Royal Birkdale: Rory on links is always dangerous, and Aaron Rai will fancy links conditions as well. If both fire, Arif could hold second or even challenge first. If neither does, Ali Pejman's lead will feel very, very comfortable.

participants.name: Kevin Young
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns alone deposited $2.43M into Kevin's account. Sam Burns! The man is a golfing ATM and Kevin had him. Third overall and climbing.
participants.blurb: Kevin Young has been quietly excellent all season: Masters winner McIlroy, then Sam Burns running a six-under final round at Shinnecock to finish solo second and bank $2.43M. Kurt Kitayama added $51K and Rory chipped in $128K. The US Open total of $2.65M moved Kevin from third to — well, third, but a much more comfortable third. Rory missed the cut at Shinnecock which hurt the floor but Burns made up for it with interest. Heading into Royal Birkdale: Burns is the kind of player who rises in links golf, and Rory McIlroy was literally born for Royal Birkdale. Two legitimate contenders. Kevin is in the conversation for overall winner if things break right.

participants.name: Matt Cavelti
participants.blurbShort: Aaron Rai won the PGA. Scottie Scheffler finished T4 at the US Open. Matt Cavelti is fourth overall and absolutely furious that Scheffler keeps finishing T4 instead of first.
participants.blurb: Matt Cavelti's season has been a lesson in almost. Aaron Rai gave him the PGA Championship. Scottie Scheffler has been excellent everywhere he's gone — second at Augusta, T4 at Shinnecock — without winning. The T4 ($920K) is real money, Tyrrell Hatton's T7 ($617K) was a welcome surprise, and Aaron Rai added $405K with a T11. The $2M US Open total moves Matt to fourth overall with $10.83M cumulative. At Royal Birkdale: Scheffler on links is genuinely unknown territory — his game is pure ball-striking, which translates, but he's not traditionally a links specialist. Rai on links? Unknown. The Open is Matt's biggest question mark of the season.

participants.name: Phil Kuzyk
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T4, Sam Burns second — Phil had both. Jumped from 10th to 5th overall. The kind of US Open that makes you annoyingly confident heading into The Open.
participants.blurb: Phil Kuzyk had the two best-positioned players in the entire pool who weren't named Wyndham Clark. Scottie Scheffler finished T4 ($920K) and Sam Burns finished second ($2.43M). Add Robert MacIntyre's T39 ($101K) and the US Open contributed $3.5M to Phil's season total. He's now fifth overall with $8.79M cumulative — a jump of five spots from his pre-US Open position. Hideki Matsuyama and Brooks Koepka were dead weight, but who cares when your top two players bank $3.35M combined. At Royal Birkdale: Burns and Scheffler keep Phil dangerous. A repeat Burns performance on links would put him within striking distance of the top three overall.

participants.name: Phil Kinninmont
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns second. Tyrrell Hatton T7. Gary Woodland T7. Jon Rahm cut the course in two at Aronimink and then immediately missed the cut at Shinnecock. Classic Rahm. Classic Phil.
participants.blurb: Phil Kinninmont had the single best collection of players who were actually in contention at Shinnecock not counting Wyndham Clark. Sam Burns finished second ($2.43M), Tyrrell Hatton T7 ($617K), Gary Woodland T7 ($617K), Matt Fitzpatrick 22nd ($230K). Jon Rahm, who delivered $1.8M at the PGA, came to Shinnecock, shot 78 in round two, and went home early. $0. Classic. Still, the $3.63M US Open total moves Phil from 16th to 6th overall — the biggest single-major leap in the pool. At Royal Birkdale: Burns on links, Hatton in Britain, and Rahm motivated to make amends. This card could be terrifying in Scotland. Phil is the pool's dark horse.

participants.name: Michael O'Callaghan
participants.blurbShort: Rory T32, Tyrrell Hatton T7 — a pleasant surprise. Viktor Hovland and Nick Taylor were spectators. Michael is 7th overall and safely holding his group lead.
participants.blurb: Michael O'Callaghan's US Open was defined by what didn't happen: Viktor Hovland missed the cut, Si Woo Kim missed the cut, Nick Taylor missed the cut. What did happen: Tyrrell Hatton came T7 out of nowhere ($617K), Rory added $128K, Hideki Matsuyama scraped $44K. The $790K US Open total is modest but Michael entered the major with such a large cumulative lead from the Masters and PGA that he's still 7th overall and group leader comfortably. At Royal Birkdale: Rory McIlroy at a links major is basically a different tournament for the rest of the pool. If Rory wins The Open, Michael has a legitimate shot at the overall title. Viktor Hovland on links is also genuinely dangerous when his head is right.

participants.name: Adam Hill
participants.blurbShort: Rory T32, Hideki T65. Jason Day withdrew before hitting a single shot at Shinnecock. Adam Hill is 8th overall and deeply unimpressed with his supporting cast.
participants.blurb: Adam Hill's Masters advantage — riding Rory McIlroy's win — has been slowly eroded by three majors of middling performance from the rest of the card. Rory at Shinnecock finished T32 ($128K), Hideki Matsuyama T65 ($44K), Jason Day withdrew, Daniel Berger and Adam Scott missed the cut, Russell Henley collapsed. The $218K US Open total is the kind of number that makes a person want to have a very frank conversation with their draft picks. Adam is 8th overall and group leader, which is genuinely impressive given the circumstances. At Royal Birkdale: Rory on links is the lifeline. Without a strong Rory performance, Adam is watching his group lead erode toward Jeff Williams.

participants.name: Jeff Williams
participants.blurbShort: Rory T32, Keegan Bradley T32, Corey Conners T23. Max Homa wasn't even in the field again. Jeff has been carrying this portfolio like a one-legged stool and somehow it's still standing.
participants.blurb: Jeff Williams has been drafting Max Homa as a core piece of his portfolio in a season where Homa apparently decided golf was optional. Not in the field at Augusta, not in the field at Shinnecock. Twice. The US Open gave Jeff Rory ($128K), Corey Conners ($181K), and Keegan Bradley ($128K) for a total of $438K. Not bad. Not good. Survivable. He's 9th overall with $7.37M cumulative, just ahead of Rod Armstrong who had an enormous US Open. At Royal Birkdale: Rory, Keegan Bradley and Corey Conners could all perform on links. If all three fire, Jeff moves. If Rory wins, Jeff could push into the top five overall.

participants.name: Rod Armstrong
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns second, Corey Conners T23, Jon Rahm cut. Rod jumped from 22nd to 10th overall in one major. Jon Rahm owes Rod Armstrong an apology.
participants.blurb: Rod Armstrong had Sam Burns and Corey Conners at Shinnecock. Sam Burns ($2.43M) played the finest Sunday round in the field. Corey Conners T23 ($181K) was steady. Robert MacIntyre T39 ($101K) added a little. Jon Rahm — who gave Rod $1.8M at Aronimink — arrived at Shinnecock, shot 68 in round one, felt good about himself, then shot 78 in round two and missed the cut. $0. Despite the Rahm betrayal, the $3.17M US Open total vaulted Rod from 22nd to 10th overall. That is a remarkable jump. At Royal Birkdale: Jon Rahm on links is almost always dangerous. Sam Burns is legitimate. Rod's chances of staying in the top ten are real.

participants.name: Sean Fairburn
participants.blurbShort: Burns T2, Fitzpatrick 22nd, Ben Griffin T17. Patrick Reed and Nicolai Hojgaard missed the cut and were judged accordingly. Sean moved from 21st to 11th. Quietly excellent.
participants.blurb: Sean Fairburn had three horses running at Shinnecock and all three delivered. Sam Burns finished second ($2.43M), Matt Fitzpatrick 22nd ($230K), Ben Griffin T17 ($280K). Patrick Reed and Nicolai Hojgaard missed the cut but their absence was barely noticed given the quality of Burns's Sunday. The $2.98M US Open total moved Sean from 21st to 11th overall — the second-biggest jump in the pool behind Phil Kinninmont. At Royal Birkdale: Burns on links is a legitimate contender. Fitzpatrick won the US Open at Brookline on pure precision — Royal Birkdale rewards that style. Sean is in the top ten conversation heading into the final major.

participants.name: Tom Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T4, Theegala T11, Justin Thomas T17, Spieth T56. A solid US Open from a card built on Scheffler's back. 12th overall and perfectly positioned for Birkdale.
participants.blurb: Tom Edstrand's six-player lineup delivered across the board at Shinnecock. Scottie Scheffler T4 ($920K), Sahith Theegala T11 ($405K), Justin Thomas T17 ($280K), Jordan Spieth T56 ($48K), Kurt Kitayama T53 ($51K). Viktor Hovland missed the cut and nobody really noticed. The $1.7M US Open total is solid without being spectacular — which is Tom's vibe all season. He's 12th overall with $6.98M. At Royal Birkdale: Scheffler is always a threat. Spieth on links is historically transformative. Thomas has won at links-adjacent courses. Tom's card actually gets better at Birkdale, not worse.

participants.name: James Patillo
participants.blurbShort: Wyndham Clark won the US Open. James Patillo had him. James also has $6.98M cumulative and is 13th overall despite Clark delivering $4.5M this week. Something has gone badly wrong in the first two majors.
participants.blurb: The James Patillo paradox is now officially documented for posterity. The man drafted the US Open champion, watched Clark deliver $4.5M, collected $4.78M total for the week — and is still 13th overall. Clark's heroics couldn't overcome the damage done by a collective zero from Ludvig Aberg, Viktor Hovland, Min Woo Lee, Shane Lowry, and Daniel Berger across three majors. The US Open major prize was Ali Pejman's because Clark was all James had while Pejman also had Hatton, Conners, and Rory. At Royal Birkdale: Clark defending is interesting. The supporting cast of mostly-European players on an English links course gives James a fighting chance at a top-ten overall finish. But he needed this $4.5M three majors ago.

participants.name: Chris Cooper
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T4, Theegala T11, Sungjae Im T43. Jon Rahm and Brooks Koepka delivered their customary nothing. Chris moved from 9th to 14th somehow. The Rahm/Koepka anchor strikes again.
participants.blurb: Chris Cooper had Scottie Scheffler and Sahith Theegala in the top twelve at Shinnecock, which should have been a triumphant week. Instead, Jon Rahm missed the cut ($0), Brooks Koepka missed the cut ($0), Min Woo Lee missed the cut ($0), leaving Scheffler ($920K), Theegala ($405K), and Sungjae Im ($72K) to carry the $1.39M total. Chris dropped from 9th to 14th overall — a painful reminder that Rahm and Koepka are taking up roster spots that could have housed, say, anyone else. At Royal Birkdale: Scheffler and Theegala are always dangerous. Spieth on links at the British Open is a recurring nightmare for opponents. If Rahm can remember how to complete 72 holes, Chris could move.

participants.name: Jamie MacDougall
participants.blurbShort: Rory T32, Brian Harman T32. JJ Spaun missed the cut as the defending champion. Marco Penge didn't play. Jamie is 15th and pretty philosophical about all of it.
participants.blurb: Jamie MacDougall's season has been a masterclass in polite disappointment. Rory McIlroy gives him the Masters earnings, then has a quiet US Open (T32, $128K). Brian Harman T32 ($128K) is steady. JJ Spaun, the defending champion, missed the cut at the course he won last year. Marco Penge withdrew with a health problem before the tournament started. Hideki Matsuyama T65. The $302K US Open total is modest. Jamie is 15th overall with $6.37M — a solid if uninspiring position. At Royal Birkdale: Rory McIlroy on a links course in the British Isles. If that doesn't excite you, nothing will. Harman won The Open. JJ Spaun exists. Jamie's best major might be the last one.

participants.name: Sean Monahan
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T4, Justin Thomas T17. Jason Day withdrew, Jake Knapp missed the cut. Sean is 16th overall and living proof that one good player can carry a lot of dead weight.
participants.blurb: Sean Monahan arrived at Shinnecock with Scottie Scheffler and a collection of supporting characters ranging from unavailable (Jason Day, withdrew) to absent (Jake Knapp, missed cut) to adequate (JT T17, Robert MacIntyre T39, Ryan Fox T23). Scheffler's $920K and Thomas's $280K did the heavy lifting for a $1.48M US Open total. Sean is 16th overall with $6.30M. The gap to the top ten is bridgeable if The Open goes well. At Royal Birkdale: Scheffler is always in the equation. Justin Thomas on an English links course is interesting. Ryan Fox is a New Zealander and genetically predisposed to links golf. Sean's card improves at Birkdale.

participants.name: David Rose
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns second. Ryo Hisatsune T43. Jon Rahm cut in round two. David Rose is 17th overall having vaulted from 25th. Rahm giveth and Rahm taketh away.
participants.blurb: David Rose's US Open story has two chapters. Chapter one: Jon Rahm shoots 68 in round one and David dares to hope. Chapter two: Jon Rahm shoots 78 in round two and David does not. Sam Burns ($2.43M) was the redemption arc — a brilliant final-round 67 at Shinnecock that moved Burns to second and Rose from 25th to 17th overall. Ryo Hisatsune T43 ($72K) was a nice bonus. The $2.57M total is one of the better US Open weeks in the pool. At Royal Birkdale: Sam Burns on links is a legitimate threat. Jon Rahm has historically been good at The Open. Sepp Straka and Nicolai Hojgaard are wildcards. David's got a real shot at the top ten if Burns keeps this form.

participants.name: Rob Pettit
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T11, Justin Thomas T17, Tyrrell Hatton T7. Three Justins and a Tyrrell. Patrick Reed and Nick Taylor missed the cut. Rob is 18th and quietly content.
participants.blurb: Rob Pettit had the pool's most elegantly named trio: two Justins and a Tyrrell. Justin Rose T11 ($405K), Tyrrell Hatton T7 ($617K), Justin Thomas T17 ($280K). Patrick Reed and Adam Scott missed the cut, Nick Taylor the same. The $1.3M US Open total is respectable and lifted Rob to 18th overall with $6.19M. Nobody is calling Rob a contender but nobody is writing him off either. At Royal Birkdale: Hatton on a links course is a different animal entirely — he has won on links and will be motivated. Rose at British Opens is historically strong. JT on links is unpredictable but talented. Rob's card gets better at Birkdale.

participants.name: Gareth McDonnell
participants.blurbShort: Xander T11, Justin Rose T11, Tyrrell Hatton T7. Shane Lowry missed the cut at a major on the island of Ireland's neighbouring island, which is deeply on brand.
participants.blurb: Gareth McDonnell had three legitimate earners at Shinnecock: Tyrrell Hatton T7 ($617K), Xander Schauffele T11 ($405K), Justin Rose T11 ($405K). Shane Lowry, the man best suited to links conditions in the entire pool, missed the cut. Marco Penge and Aldrich Potgieter were not in the field. The $1.43M US Open total moves Gareth to 19th overall with $5.71M — a solid position but a long way from the top ten. At Royal Birkdale: Shane Lowry on links is the obvious wild card — when he's right, he's unbeatable on this type of course. Hatton and Rose both thrive in Britain. If all three fire at Birkdale, Gareth has a genuine run at the top fifteen.

participants.name: Chris Kleaman
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T11, Ben Griffin T17, Cameron Young T43. Russell Henley shot 80 in round three. Chris is 20th overall and has developed a healthy distance from the Henley situation.
participants.blurb: Chris Kleaman's US Open was defined by one R3 collapse and one unexpected bright spot. Justin Rose T11 ($405K) and Ben Griffin T17 ($280K) delivered. Russell Henley, who entered the week as a legitimate contender, shot a third-round 80 and tumbled to T65 ($44K). Cameron Young T43 ($72K) was steady-ish. Jacob Bridgeman T39 ($101K) was a small bonus. The $906K total keeps Chris at 20th overall with $5.71M. At Royal Birkdale: Rose on a links course is historically very good. Henley on links is — well, we'll see, given Shinnecock. Chris needs one of his players to win The Open to make a serious move up the standings.

participants.name: Darren Moscovitch
participants.blurbShort: Sam Burns second. Corey Conners T23. Akshay Bhatia T17. Viktor Hovland missed the cut. Darren jumped from 43rd to 21st. The best major of his season and nobody noticed because Ali Pejman was collecting $5.4M.
participants.blurb: Darren Moscovitch quietly had an exceptional US Open and will be insufferable about it at exactly the wrong moment. Sam Burns finished second ($2.43M), Corey Conners T23 ($181K), Akshay Bhatia T17 ($280K), Ludvig Aberg T17 ($280K). Viktor Hovland and Sepp Straka missed the cut. The $3.17M total is the fourth-largest US Open haul in the pool. Darren vaulted from 43rd all the way to 21st overall — the third-biggest jump in the pool. At Royal Birkdale: Burns in form, Bhatia on links (interesting), Aberg on links (very interesting). Darren could keep climbing if the form holds.

participants.name: Jimmy Richmond
participants.blurbShort: Ludvig Aberg T17, Jordan Spieth T56, Brian Harman T32, Jacob Bridgeman T39. Jon Rahm, Jake Knapp and Hideki all cut or absent. Jimmy stays 22nd and philosophically accepts the Rahm situation.
participants.blurb: Jimmy Richmond's US Open was a minor comeback story after the Rahm disaster at Aronimink. Ludvig Aberg T17 ($280K), Brian Harman T32 ($128K), Jacob Bridgeman T39 ($101K), Jordan Spieth T56 ($48K). Jon Rahm cut, Jake Knapp absent, Hideki Matsuyama T65. The $476K US Open total is modest but at least Aberg reversed the R3 horror show from Saturday. Jimmy stays 22nd overall. At Royal Birkdale: Aberg on a links course is a genuine threat — the Swede drives it beautifully and his iron play suits precision links work. Jordan Spieth at British Opens is a documented phenomenon. Jimmy's best shot at a big week is at Birkdale.

participants.name: Jon Greyell
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T4 and Keegan Bradley T32. Viktor Hovland, Cantlay, Si Woo and Nick Taylor all missed the cut. Jon's entire US Open was Scottie Scheffler, and Scottie Scheffler did not win. He's 23rd.
participants.blurb: Jon Greyell's card at Shinnecock was Scottie Scheffler and five passengers. Scheffler T4 ($920K), Keegan Bradley T32 ($128K), and then a parade of missed cuts from Viktor Hovland, Patrick Cantlay, Si Woo Kim, and Nick Taylor. The $1.04M total is almost entirely Scheffler. Jon is 23rd overall with $5.27M — a respectable position built almost entirely on one man's shoulders. At Royal Birkdale: Scheffler is always the horse. Keegan Bradley on links is a wildcard but potentially interesting. The rest of the card needs to remember how to play 36 holes. Jon's ceiling at Birkdale is determined entirely by whether Scheffler decides to win a British Open.

participants.name: Mike Richmond
participants.blurbShort: Xander T11, Maverick McNealy T32, Ludvig Aberg T17. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren missed the cut. Russell Henley collapsed to T65. Mike is 24th and calling this a moral victory.
participants.blurb: Mike Richmond had Xander Schauffele T11 ($405K), Ludvig Aberg T17 ($280K), and Maverick McNealy T32 ($128K) doing reasonable work at Shinnecock. Russell Henley fell to T65 after a brutal round three. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren missed the cut. The $860K US Open total is steady without being exciting. Mike is 24th overall with $5.19M. At Royal Birkdale: Schauffele on a links course is always competitive. Aberg on links should be excellent. Maverick McNealy on links is an unknown quantity but the man has been full of surprises this major season. Mike's card is legitimately interesting at Birkdale.

participants.name: Nils Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Scheffler T4, Keegan Bradley T32, Brian Harman T32. Bryson missed the cut for the billionth time. Nils is 25th and has accepted the Bryson situation as a feature, not a bug.
participants.blurb: Nils Edstrand drafted Bryson DeChambeau and has been rewarded with the same number of made cuts as Nils's actual handicap — zero. Bryson missed the cut again. Alex Noren and Harris English also absent from the weekend. What saved the week: Scheffler T4 ($920K), Brian Harman T32 ($128K), Keegan Bradley T32 ($128K). The $1.17M US Open total is perfectly adequate. Nils is 25th overall. At Royal Birkdale: Scheffler is the horse. Harman has won The Open Championship — at Hoylake, admittedly, but the form is there. Bradley on links is a curiosity. Bryson at a British links course is either spectacular or a complete disaster. Probably the latter.

participants.name: Jay Barre
participants.blurbShort: Maverick McNealy T32, Justin Rose T11, Cameron Young T43. Ryan Gerard missed the cut. Jay is 26th overall and has discovered that the sleeper pick who delivers is infinitely more satisfying than the star who doesn't.
participants.blurb: Jay Barre had the pleasant experience of watching Maverick McNealy — one of the quieter picks in the pool — deliver a T32 ($128K) while Justin Rose T11 ($405K) and Cameron Young T43 ($72K) added supporting income. Chris Gotterup T43 ($72K), Jacob Bridgeman T39 ($101K) filled out the card. Ryan Gerard missed the cut. The $781K US Open total is respectable. Jay is 26th overall with $4.99M. At Royal Birkdale: Justin Rose on a links course is almost always interesting. Cameron Young has the trajectory of a player who will win a major. McNealy is a wildcard. Jay's ceiling is higher than his current position suggests.

participants.name: Stephen Roberts
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick 22nd, Morikawa T17, McNealy T32, Woodland T7, Griffin T17. Five of six made the cut and earned. Cam Smith was the one blemish. Stephen is 27th and the most underrated card in the pool.
participants.blurb: Stephen Roberts had the most consistently productive card at Shinnecock that nobody is talking about. Gary Woodland T7 ($617K), Matt Fitzpatrick 22nd ($230K), Collin Morikawa T17 ($280K), Maverick McNealy T32 ($128K), Ben Griffin T17 ($280K). Cam Smith missed the cut. Five players earning real money at one of the hardest courses in golf is exceptional roster management. The $1.54M US Open total moves Stephen to 27th overall. He deserves better. At Royal Birkdale: Fitzpatrick at Birkdale is interesting given his precision-iron style. Morikawa is always a factor at major championships. Woodland and McNealy on links are wildcards. Stephen could genuinely move ten places at The Open.

participants.name: Craig Langdon
participants.blurbShort: Morikawa T17, Gary Woodland T7, Brian Harman T32, Aberg T17. A quietly excellent US Open from Craig, overshadowed entirely by Ali Pejman's $5.4M. Craig is 28th and deserves a trophy for consistency.
participants.blurb: Craig Langdon had four players contributing at Shinnecock: Collin Morikawa T17 ($280K), Gary Woodland T7 ($617K), Brian Harman T32 ($128K), Ludvig Aberg T17 ($280K). Daniel Berger missed the cut and Russell Henley continued his collapse to T65 ($44K). The $1.35M US Open total is solid. Craig is 28th overall — a perfectly pleasant position for a man whose card punched above its weight all week. At Royal Birkdale: Morikawa is always dangerous at majors. Aberg on links should excel. Brian Harman is a former Open champion. Craig could finish this season very respectably.

participants.name: Hans Edstrand
participants.blurbShort: Xander T11, Morikawa T17. Cam Smith and Alex Noren missed the cut. Hans quietly had one of the better US Opens in the bottom half of the standings. He is 29th and chronically undersold.
participants.blurb: Hans Edstrand has developed a pattern this season: underestimated, quietly excellent, perpetually mid-table. Xander Schauffele T11 ($405K), Collin Morikawa T17 ($280K), Kurt Kitayama T53 ($51K). Cam Smith, Alex Noren and Nick Taylor all missed the cut. The $738K US Open total moves Hans to 29th overall with $4.43M. At Royal Birkdale: Schauffele on a links course is interesting — he's a major champion who plays well in all conditions. Morikawa won The Open at Royal St George's. Hans has two major winners on his card and nobody is paying attention.

participants.name: Mark Kaufmann
participants.blurbShort: Xander T11, Corey Conners T23, Tyrrell Hatton T7, Brian Harman T32. Four live players and a solid result. Harris English missed the cut, which at this point is expected.
participants.blurb: Mark Kaufmann had depth at Shinnecock: Tyrrell Hatton T7 ($617K), Xander Schauffele T11 ($405K), Corey Conners T23 ($181K), Brian Harman T32 ($128K), Robert MacIntyre T39 ($101K). Harris English missed the cut, which has become as reliable as the tides. The $1.43M US Open total moves Mark to 30th overall with $4.39M — a respectable improvement. At Royal Birkdale: Hatton in Britain, Schauffele as always, Harman as a former Open champion, Conners on links. This card is built for Birkdale. Mark has a legitimate shot at a top-fifteen finish overall if The Open delivers.

participants.name: JP Wright
participants.blurbShort: Maverick McNealy T32, Gary Woodland T7, Tyrrell Hatton T7, Spieth T56. Bryson and Brooks delivered their joint zero. JP is 31st and has made peace with his LIV picks.
participants.blurb: JP Wright's US Open will be remembered for what Tyrrell Hatton and Gary Woodland did (T7 each, $617K each) and what Bryson DeChambeau and Brooks Koepka didn't (missed cut, $0 each). Maverick McNealy T32 ($128K) and Jordan Spieth T56 ($48K) rounded out the week. The $1.41M total is real and moves JP to 31st overall with $4.27M. At Royal Birkdale: Hatton is electric on a links course and will fancy his chances. Woodland is capable. Spieth at The Open is historically one of the best bets in golf. JP might be sitting on a top-twenty finish at Birkdale.

participants.name: Dino Konasewich
participants.blurbShort: Maverick McNealy T32, Justin Rose T11, Cameron Young T43. Shane Lowry and JJ Spaun cut. Dino is 32nd and reasonably satisfied with his position for a former champion.
participants.blurb: Dino Konasewich won this thing in 2017 and has spent the subsequent nine years reminding everyone about it at every possible opportunity. The US Open gave him Justin Rose T11 ($405K), Maverick McNealy T32 ($128K), Cameron Young T43 ($72K). Shane Lowry, JJ Spaun and Rasmus Hojgaard all missed the cut. The $607K US Open total is modest. Dino is 32nd overall with $4.24M. At Royal Birkdale: Justin Rose at a British links is almost always top twenty. Cameron Young has the talent to win a major. Shane Lowry on links — if he makes the cut this time — is devastating. Dino could close the season respectably.

participants.name: Hugh Cooper Jr.
participants.blurbShort: Xander T11, Fitzpatrick 22nd, Conners T23, Akshay Bhatia T17. Patrick Reed and Si Woo Kim cut. Hugh had the best four-player cluster in the bottom half of the standings. 33rd overall.
participants.blurb: Hugh Cooper Jr. is quietly having the most balanced season of anyone outside the top twenty. At Shinnecock: Xander Schauffele T11 ($405K), Akshay Bhatia T17 ($280K), Corey Conners T23 ($181K), Matt Fitzpatrick 22nd ($230K). Patrick Reed and Si Woo Kim missed the cut but it barely registered. The $1.09M US Open total moves Hugh to 33rd overall with $4.2M — genuinely competitive. At Royal Birkdale: Fitzpatrick's precision iron play is tailor-made for links golf. Schauffele is always in the mix. Conners on links is interesting. Hugh's floor at Birkdale is higher than his ranking suggests.

participants.name: Kit Matkaluk
participants.blurbShort: Justin Thomas T17, Cameron Young T43, Chris Gotterup T43. Jacob Bridgeman T39. Patrick Cantlay missed the cut. Kit is 34th and the reigning champion, a fact his current position makes awkward.
participants.blurb: Kit Matkaluk won this pool in 2023 and is currently 34th overall, which is a trajectory that tells its own story. At Shinnecock: Justin Thomas T17 ($280K), Cameron Young T43 ($72K), Chris Gotterup T43 ($72K), Jacob Bridgeman T39 ($101K). Patrick Cantlay missed the cut. Marco Penge didn't play. The $528K US Open total is modest. Kit is 34th with $3.98M. At Royal Birkdale: Justin Thomas on links is always interesting. Cameron Young feels perpetually like he's about to win a major. Kit needs an unexpected hero among his picks — the kind of thing that happens at The Open more than anywhere else.

participants.name: Rob Snowden
participants.blurbShort: Collin Morikawa T17, Gary Woodland T7, Cameron Young T43. Jake Knapp absent, Si Woo and Matt McCarty cut. Rob is 35th and grateful that at least Morikawa exists.
participants.blurb: Rob Snowden's US Open can be summarised as: Collin Morikawa ($280K), Gary Woodland ($617K), Cameron Young ($72K) working, and Jake Knapp not in the field, Si Woo Kim cut, Matt McCarty cut. The $970K total moves Rob from 35th to — well, 35th, just a more comfortable 35th. At Royal Birkdale: Morikawa won The Open at Royal St George's in 2021 and plays links golf beautifully. Woodland is always capable. Cameron Young on links could be explosive. Rob's best major of the season might yet come at Birkdale.

participants.name: Jamie Finkbeiner
participants.blurbShort: Xander T11, Corey Conners T23, Akshay Bhatia T17, Cameron Young T43. Sepp Straka and Alex Noren cut. Jamie is 36th and operating well below his actual quality.
participants.blurb: Jamie Finkbeiner's card at Shinnecock was legitimately good and his position in the standings (36th) does not reflect it. Xander Schauffele T11 ($405K), Akshay Bhatia T17 ($280K), Corey Conners T23 ($181K), Cameron Young T43 ($72K). Sepp Straka and Alex Noren missed the cut. The $940K US Open total is solid. At Royal Birkdale: Schauffele's links record is improving. Bhatia on a British links is a genuine unknown — but interesting. Conners on links could surprise. Young at The Open is a name people will start talking about if he keeps performing. Jamie's best finish of the season may come at Birkdale.

participants.name: Nigel Coleclough
participants.blurbShort: It's literally your Cup. You are 37th. Justin Thomas T17, Ben Griffin T17, Jordan Spieth T56. JJ Spaun missed the cut as defending champion. Please see yourself to the podium anyway.
participants.blurb: The founder of the feast is 37th at his own tournament. Nigel Coleclough — who created the Coleclough Cup, named the Coleclough Cup after himself, and hosts the Coleclough Cup — sits 37th of 56 participants at the three-quarter mark of the 2026 edition of the Coleclough Cup. Justin Thomas T17 ($280K), Ben Griffin T17 ($280K), Jordan Spieth T56 ($48K), Hideki Matsuyama T65 ($44K). JJ Spaun missed the cut. Jake Knapp didn't play. The $655K US Open total keeps Nigel at 37th with $3.84M. At Royal Birkdale: Spieth at The Open is the obvious lifeline. JT and Griffin could contribute. Nigel needs The Open to do something spectacular to avoid a very awkward trophy presentation.

participants.name: Ted Reid
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T11, Justin Thomas T17, MacIntyre T39, Spieth T56. Patrick Reed and Harris English cut. Ted is 38th and philosophically at peace with his supporting cast.
participants.blurb: Ted Reid's US Open delivered steadily without brilliance. Tommy Fleetwood T11 ($405K), Justin Thomas T17 ($280K), Robert MacIntyre T39 ($101K), Jordan Spieth T56 ($48K). Patrick Reed and Harris English both missed the cut. The $837K total keeps Ted at 38th overall with $3.83M. At Royal Birkdale: Fleetwood is always a contender at British majors and has been knocking on the door for years. Thomas on links is interesting. MacIntyre won the Canadian Open and has the game for links conditions. Spieth at The Open. Ted's card is legitimately competitive at Birkdale.

participants.name: Dionne Delesalle
participants.blurbShort: Collin Morikawa T17, Justin Rose T11. Jason Day withdrew, Adam Scott and Daniel Berger cut. Two workers, four ghosts. Dionne is 39th and rooting very hard for Morikawa.
participants.blurb: Dionne Delesalle had two players earn real money at Shinnecock and four that didn't. Collin Morikawa T17 ($280K), Justin Rose T11 ($405K). Jason Day withdrew, Adam Scott cut, Nicolai Hojgaard cut, Daniel Berger cut. The $686K total keeps Dionne at 39th with $3.78M. It could have been much worse and also could have been much better, which is a perfectly 39th-place-overall kind of observation. At Royal Birkdale: Morikawa won The Open in 2021. Rose at links majors is reliable. The remaining four picks will need to break their established habit of not finishing tournaments.

participants.name: Bryson Milley
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T11, Collin Morikawa T17, Keegan Bradley T32. Patrick Cantlay, Cam Smith, and Max Homa collectively remembered that golf exists but didn't attend. Bryson is 40th.
participants.blurb: Bryson Milley — not to be confused with Bryson DeChambeau, who would have missed the cut anyway — had three solid players at Shinnecock. Tommy Fleetwood T11 ($405K), Collin Morikawa T17 ($280K), Keegan Bradley T32 ($128K). Patrick Cantlay and Cam Smith missed the cut. Max Homa was not in the field for the second major running, which at this point qualifies as a lifestyle choice. The $815K total keeps Bryson M. at 40th overall with $3.72M. At Royal Birkdale: Fleetwood on a British links is always dangerous. Morikawa is a former champion. Bradley on links is genuinely unknown but intriguing. Cam Smith on links is potentially excellent — if he's in the field this time.

participants.name: David Puddicombe
participants.blurbShort: All six players missed the cut, withdrew, or didn't play. Three majors, three complete shutouts. $0 US Open earnings confirmed. This is not a run of bad luck. This is a lifestyle.
participants.blurb: David Puddicombe has now achieved the rarest feat in Coleclough Cup history: three consecutive majors with zero pool earnings from any player. Jon Rahm cut. Bryson DeChambeau cut. Patrick Reed cut. Daniel Berger cut. Jason Day withdrew. Sepp Straka cut. $0. This is not bad luck anymore. This is the Puddicombe Theorem: given any field of 156 golfers, David's six picks will find the exit. The $3.44M cumulative is entirely from the first two majors (Rahm $1.8M at the PGA, Reed at the Masters). At Royal Birkdale: Jon Rahm on a links course is one of golf's great spectacles. So is Bryson. At least The Open gives David a chance at a genuinely redemptive week — assuming his picks can locate the first tee.

participants.name: Stu Louie
participants.blurbShort: Xander T11, Akshay Bhatia T17, Jordan Spieth T56. Brooks Koepka cut at +10, Min Woo Lee cut, Nicolai Hojgaard cut. Stu is 42nd and quietly happy Xander exists.
participants.blurb: Stu Louie's US Open was carried by Xander Schauffele T11 ($405K) and Akshay Bhatia T17 ($280K). Jordan Spieth T56 ($48K) was a modest contributor. Brooks Koepka missed the cut with a ten-over total that felt like a personal statement. Min Woo Lee and Nicolai Hojgaard also cut. The $735K total keeps Stu at 42nd with $3.40M. At Royal Birkdale: Schauffele is always a threat. Spieth at The Open is essentially its own storyline — he's finished second twice and won once. Bhatia on links is a genuine wildcard. Stu could move ten places at Birkdale if these three fire.

participants.name: Mark Trotzuk
participants.blurbShort: Justin Rose T11, Ryan Fox T23, Kurt Kitayama T53. Jason Day withdrew, JJ Spaun and Shane Lowry cut. Mark is 43rd and getting quite good at finding the silver lining.
participants.blurb: Mark Trotzuk had a mixed bag at Shinnecock. Justin Rose T11 ($405K) was the headline. Ryan Fox T23 ($181K) was a pleasant surprise for the Kiwi. Kurt Kitayama T53 ($51K) added something. Jason Day withdrew. Shane Lowry, JJ Spaun, and Ryan Gerard all missed the cut. The $457K US Open total is the card punching at around half its potential weight. Mark is 43rd overall with $3.39M. At Royal Birkdale: Justin Rose loves links conditions. Shane Lowry is one of the most dangerous players in the world at British opens. Jason Day — if healthy — on a links course can be special. Mark's Birkdale ceiling is genuinely higher than 43rd.

participants.name: Chris Morrison
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T11, Sahith Theegala T11. Patrick Reed and Adam Scott cut, Russell Henley collapsed. Chris is 44th and has decided Theegala is his guy going forward.
participants.blurb: Chris Morrison had the pleasant surprise of Sahith Theegala T11 ($405K) alongside Tommy Fleetwood T11 ($405K) — two of the cleanest Sunday earners on the board. Patrick Reed and Adam Scott both missed the cut. Russell Henley shot 80 in round three and disappeared down the standings. Sepp Straka cut. The $856K US Open total is solid for 44th place. Chris moves up from 44th to — 44th, more securely. At Royal Birkdale: Fleetwood at a British major is always compelling. Theegala on links is an interesting proposition — he's consistent, his ball-striking is elite, and links conditions might suit him. Adam Scott at 46 on a links course is a wildcard that occasionally pays off.

participants.name: Geoff Stollery
participants.blurbShort: Theegala T11, Fitzpatrick 22nd, Bhatia T17, Ryo Hisatsune T43. Nicolai Hojgaard cut. Geoff had four players contributing and jumped twelve places. The lowest Masters total has become the best US Open.
participants.blurb: Geoff Stollery posted the lowest Masters earnings of anyone in the pool and spent two majors building himself back up. At Shinnecock: Sahith Theegala T11 ($405K), Matt Fitzpatrick 22nd ($230K), Akshay Bhatia T17 ($280K), Ryo Hisatsune T43 ($72K), Ludvig Aberg T17 ($280K). Nicolai Hojgaard cut. The $1.27M US Open total vaulted Geoff from 48th to 45th overall. Not the most dramatic jump but a genuine improvement. At Royal Birkdale: Aberg on a links course is one of the most intriguing storylines heading into The Open — the Swede is an exceptional ball-striker. Fitzpatrick's precision game translates. Theegala is unpredictable but talented. Geoff ends the season better than he started it.

participants.name: Aaron Watson
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T11, Sungjae Im T43, Nico Echavarria T56. Jon Rahm cut, Shane Lowry cut, Viktor Hovland cut. Aaron is 46th and wishing he'd drafted literally anyone else instead of three players who dislike Shinnecock.
participants.blurb: Aaron Watson's US Open was the story of one good player (Tommy Fleetwood T11, $405K) and a lot of people who found ways not to make the weekend. Jon Rahm cut, Shane Lowry cut, Viktor Hovland cut. Sungjae Im T43 ($72K) and Nico Echavarria T56 ($48K) contributed modestly. The $527K total keeps Aaron at 46th overall with $3.16M. At Royal Birkdale: Tommy Fleetwood is one of the most exciting players to watch at British links majors and has been agonisingly close before. Jon Rahm on links is always a factor. Shane Lowry — at a British Open, on a links, in Britain — is dangerous if he can simply finish two rounds.

participants.name: Chris Ellison
participants.blurbShort: Fleetwood T11, Morikawa T17, Niemann T7. Shane Lowry cut, Alex Noren cut. Chris had three legitimate earners and climbed to 47th. A Shinnecock redemption arc.
participants.blurb: Chris Ellison's US Open was defined by Collin Morikawa T17 ($280K), Tommy Fleetwood T11 ($405K), and Joaquin Niemann T7 ($617K). Shane Lowry and Alex Noren missed the cut. Stephan Jaeger was not in the field. The $1.3M US Open total is one of the better returns among the bottom-half participants. Chris climbs to 47th overall with $3.14M. At Royal Birkdale: Fleetwood on a British links is always must-watch. Morikawa is a former champion. Niemann is a LIV player whose links record at The Open is genuinely intriguing. Chris could finish the season much better than 47th.

participants.name: Chris MacDougall
participants.blurbShort: Akshay Bhatia T17, Robert MacIntyre T39, Cameron Young T43. Shane Lowry and Jake Knapp cut. Chris is 48th and riding a modest comeback from the worst PGA Championship in the pool.
participants.blurb: Chris MacDougall had the worst PGA Championship in the entire pool and has been quietly clawing back ever since. Akshay Bhatia T17 ($280K), Robert MacIntyre T39 ($101K), Cameron Young T43 ($72K), Jacob Bridgeman T39 ($101K). Shane Lowry and Jake Knapp missed the cut. The $557K US Open total is a step in the right direction. Chris is 48th overall with $2.63M. At Royal Birkdale: MacIntyre is Scottish and capable of a big week on a links course. Bhatia on links is unknown but interesting. Cameron Young at The Open has genuine upside. The hole is deep but The Open is the most unpredictable major of the year.

participants.name: Tim Kehler
participants.blurbShort: Fitzpatrick 22nd, Keegan Bradley T32, Ben Griffin T17. JJ Spaun, Alex Noren, Ryan Gerard all cut. Tim is 49th and has found serenity in the knowledge that Fitzpatrick always makes the weekend.
participants.blurb: Tim Kehler's season can be described as Matt Fitzpatrick and friends. Fitzpatrick 22nd ($230K), Ben Griffin T17 ($280K), Keegan Bradley T32 ($128K). JJ Spaun, Alex Noren and Ryan Gerard all missed the cut. The $639K total moves Tim to 49th overall with $2.45M. At Royal Birkdale: Fitzpatrick's game is built for links — precision iron play, patient course management, impeccable wedge work. Bradley on links is an interesting dark horse. Ben Griffin has been quietly excellent all season. Tim's top-forty finish overall is achievable if Fitzpatrick goes low at Birkdale.

participants.name: Fraser Reid
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T11, Keegan Bradley T32. JJ Spaun, Patrick Cantlay, Harris English, Max Homa all absent or cut. Fraser is 50th and grateful that at least two players remembered to play golf.
participants.blurb: Fraser Reid's US Open was two horses and a lot of empty space. Tommy Fleetwood T11 ($405K), Keegan Bradley T32 ($128K). JJ Spaun, Patrick Cantlay, Harris English — all cut. Max Homa — not in the field, again. The $534K US Open total keeps Fraser at 50th overall with $2.41M. At Royal Birkdale: Fleetwood at a British links major is always compelling. Keegan Bradley on links is an interesting proposition — the Ryder Cup captain has shown he can compete in varied conditions. Max Homa will presumably be in the field at The Open, which is the lowest possible bar. Fraser needs an Open miracle.

participants.name: Tom Puddicombe
participants.blurbShort: Tommy Fleetwood T11, Sungjae Im T43, Justin Thomas T17. Four players survived the week. His brother David had zero. The family dinner will be interesting.
participants.blurb: Tom Puddicombe had the considerable satisfaction of watching his brother David fail to contribute a single dollar in three consecutive majors while Tom himself had Tommy Fleetwood T11 ($405K), Justin Thomas T17 ($280K), Sungjae Im T43 ($72K), and Robert MacIntyre T39 ($101K) contributing at Shinnecock. The $861K US Open total moves Tom to 51st overall with $2.24M. The family dynamic at the Puddicombe table must be extraordinary. At Royal Birkdale: Fleetwood is always competitive on a British links. JT on links is interesting. MacIntyre is Scottish. Sungjae Im on links is a genuine wildcard. Tom could close the gap to 45th or so if The Open delivers. David will presumably have all his picks withdraw on the eve of the tournament.

participants.name: Kevin Spooner
participants.blurbShort: Brian Harman T32. Bryson cut, Patrick Reed cut, Min Woo Lee cut, Si Woo Kim cut. Hideki T65. Kevin is 52nd and has constructed a card entirely around players who dislike completing golf tournaments.
participants.blurb: Kevin Spooner drafted a lineup that collectively decided Shinnecock was optional. Bryson DeChambeau cut, Patrick Reed cut, Min Woo Lee cut, Si Woo Kim cut. Brian Harman T32 ($128K) was the sole bright spot. Hideki Matsuyama T65 ($44K) contributed modestly. The $173K US Open total is one of the smallest in the pool and not undeserved. Kevin is 52nd overall with $2.17M. At Royal Birkdale: Brian Harman won The Open at Hoylake in 2023. Hideki on links can occasionally surprise. The rest of the card has shown a consistent tendency to miss cuts that is not going to be reversed by switching venues to Scotland.

participants.name: Chris Gibbins
participants.blurbShort: Keegan Bradley T32, Michael Kim T43, Jordan Spieth T56. Jason Day withdrew, Bryson cut, Cantlay cut. Chris is 53rd and deeply invested in Jordan Spieth finding his best game at Birkdale.
participants.blurb: Chris Gibbins had three players earn money at Shinnecock but the combined total tells the story. Keegan Bradley T32 ($128K), Michael Kim T43 ($72K), Jordan Spieth T56 ($48K). Jason Day withdrew, Bryson DeChambeau cut, Patrick Cantlay cut. The $249K US Open total is one of the smaller hauls in the pool. Chris is 53rd overall with $2.16M. At Royal Birkdale: Jordan Spieth at The Open Championship is one of golf's most reliable storylines — the man has finished second twice, won once, and contended at almost every edition since his debut. If Spieth goes low at Birkdale, Chris makes a significant move. That is Chris's entire plan and it is not a bad one.

participants.name: Theo Ong
participants.blurbShort: Matt Fitzpatrick 22nd, Jacob Bridgeman T39. JJ Spaun, Jake Knapp, Si Woo Kim, Rickie Fowler all cut. Theo is 54th and Matt Fitzpatrick deserves better supporting cast members.
participants.blurb: Theo Ong's season rests entirely on Matt Fitzpatrick's willing shoulders. Fitzpatrick 22nd ($230K), Jacob Bridgeman T39 ($101K). JJ Spaun cut, Jake Knapp absent, Si Woo Kim cut, Rickie Fowler cut. The $332K US Open total keeps Theo at 54th with $1.83M. Fitzpatrick has been the only consistent earner across three majors for Theo, which is a tribute to the Englishman's reliability and an indictment of the rest of the card's collective reliability. At Royal Birkdale: Fitzpatrick on a links course is genuinely exciting given his skill set. The rest of the card needs to do something. Anything. Theo finishes the season at Birkdale hoping for a miracle from four players who have mastered the art of contributing nothing.

participants.name: Russell Ball
participants.blurbShort: Maverick McNealy T32, Chris Gotterup T43, Jacob Bridgeman T39. Bryson cut, both Hojgaards cut. Russell is 55th and has drafted the pool's three best players that nobody had heard of in January.
participants.blurb: Russell Ball's season can be summarised as: the players nobody wanted are carrying the players everyone wanted. Maverick McNealy T32 ($128K), Chris Gotterup T43 ($72K), Jacob Bridgeman T39 ($101K). Bryson DeChambeau cut, Nicolai and Rasmus Hojgaard both cut. The $303K US Open total keeps Russell at 55th overall with $1.73M. The cumulative does him no favours but the US Open shows he's been finding value in unexpected places all season. At Royal Birkdale: Maverick McNealy on a links course is genuinely interesting — the man has been the pool's breakout player of the US Open. Gotterup and Bridgeman on links are unknowns. Bryson at The Open is always appointment television.

participants.name: Chris Smiley
participants.blurbShort: Akshay Bhatia T17. Bryson cut, Brooks cut, Min Woo Lee cut, Jason Day WD, Marco Penge absent. Chris is dead last with $1.48M and the distinction of having drafted exactly one player who finished a golf tournament.
participants.blurb: Chris Smiley is last. Not nearly last. Last. $1.48M cumulative from three majors, almost entirely from Brooks Koepka's Masters showing and a modest PGA contribution. At Shinnecock: Akshay Bhatia T17 ($280K) was the sole survivor. Bryson DeChambeau cut. Brooks Koepka cut at +10. Min Woo Lee cut. Jason Day withdrew. Marco Penge was not in the field all season due to health issues. The $280K US Open total is the second-smallest in the pool. Chris is last. At Royal Birkdale: Bryson DeChambeau at a British links course is one of golf's most entertaining spectacles — whether he contends or creates chaos or both. Brooks Koepka is a former major champion. Someone on this card is going to eventually make a cut and it is going to feel like a victory lap.
